weather_report:
Mid-morning in Cottesloe feels open and clear, the kind of day where the light drifts through scattered cloud and lays a gentle sheen on the sea. The air is easy to breathe, edges of salt and eucalyptus, and the horizon sits clean and steady. It is mostly sunny now and will stay that way through the heart of the day, with passing patches of cloud that soften the glare without really taming it.

Expect a warm, agreeable afternoon with good visibility and a UV index that rises quickly toward midday; the sun will bite even when veiled. Dress light and breathable, add a brimmed hat and sunglasses, and treat sunscreen as part of the uniform. A short wander along the foreshore or a quiet loop through the neighborhood suits the mood; fetch bread, linger over a coffee under an awning, hang a wash so it dries in an hour, or read by an open window where the breeze finds you. Save longer stints in full sun for earlier or later, and let the day unspool at an easy pace.
